description: Stellarium Script Snippets Library - Complete collection of ready-to-use code snippets for various astronomical tasks

loops.name: Loops
loops.description: Various loop structures for repetitive tasks

items.name: Basic For Loop
items.desc: Repeat code block a specific number of times

items.name: Loop Through Array
items.desc: Iterate through array of celestial objects

items.name: Smooth Zoom Loop
items.desc: Gradual smooth zoom in/out animation

items.name: Full 360° Rotation
items.desc: Complete circle rotation for panorama effect

items.name: Altitude Scan
items.desc: Scan from horizon to zenith at fixed azimuth

items.name: Time Lapse Loop
items.desc: Loop that advances time and records data

conditionals.name: Conditionals
conditionals.description: Conditional statements for decision making in scripts

items.name: Check Object Visibility
items.desc: Check if celestial object is above horizon

items.name: Check Plugin Availability
items.desc: Check if a plugin is loaded before using it

items.name: Detect Day/Night
items.desc: Determine day/night based on Sun altitude

items.name: Compare Object Brightness
items.desc: Compare brightness of two celestial objects

variables.name: Variables & Save/Restore
variables.description: Variable management and state preservation techniques

items.name: Save & Restore Settings
items.desc: Save settings, change temporarily, then restore

items.name: Store Object Information
items.desc: Store object data in variables for later use

functions.name: Functions
functions.description: Custom function definitions for reusable code

items.name: Wait Until Function
items.desc: Generic function to wait for any condition

items.name: Get Moon Phase String (Accurate)
items.desc: Convert moon illumination to phase name accurately using age or built-in phase-name property

items.name: Get Moon Phase with Detailed Debug Output
items.desc: Display detailed moon information including phase, illumination, age, waning status, distance, magnitude, altitude, azimuth, and horizon visibility

items.name: Format Coordinates Display
items.desc: Utility functions for coordinate formatting

items.name: Display Message Sequence
items.desc: Display multiple messages sequentially on screen

tours.name: Tours & Navigation
tours.description: Pre-built tours for exploring celestial objects

items.name: Solar System Tour
items.desc: Complete tour of planets in solar system

items.name: Constellation Tour (Enhanced)
items.desc: Enhanced cinematic tour of major constellations with smooth transitions, art visualization, i18n support, and automatic restoration of original settings

items.name: Messier Marathon
items.desc: Tour of popular Messier deep-sky objects

events.name: Event Simulations
events.description: Simulate astronomical events like eclipses and conjunctions

items.name: Eclipse Simulator
items.desc: Simulate a solar eclipse over time

items.name: Planet Conjunction Finder
items.desc: Find when two planets are closest in the sky

data-collection.name: Data Collection & Export
data-collection.description: Collect astronomical data and export to files

items.name: Solar Altitude Tracker
items.desc: Track sun altitude and export to CSV

items.name: Night Sky Report
items.desc: Generate complete observation report

visual-effects.name: Visual Effects & Display
visual-effects.description: Visual effects for screen labels and displays

items.name: Fade Effect
items.desc: Fade in/out effects for screen labels

items.name: Screenshot Sequence
items.desc: Capture screenshots of multiple objects

advanced.name: Advanced Examples
advanced.description: Complex examples for advanced script development

items.name: Observing Plan Generator
items.desc: Generate custom observing plan based on visibility

items.name: Display Comprehensive Object Information
items.desc: Display comprehensive information about any celestial object with formatted tables including coordinates, magnitude, distance, visibility, rise/set times, and object-specific properties (stars, double stars, solar system)

items.name: Natural Sky Reset (Direct/Native Method)
items.desc: Reset Stellarium to natural sky using eval() with sequential command execution and error handling. Restores time, view, landscape, atmosphere, planets, stars, nebulae, grids, GUI, and projection mode to default settings.

items.name: Natural Sky Reset (Function Constructor Method)
items.desc: Alternative method using Function() constructor for safer execution. Same functionality as the direct method but with better security. Useful for production scripts where eval() might be restricted.
